key_insight: High-velocity air-fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that still retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This enables functional smart coatings on steel components for wear- and actuation-related applications where conventional ceramic coatings cannot provide shape-memory behavior.
method: Sprayed 100–300 μm NiTi coatings on mild steel using four HVAF parameter sets.
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response.
Compared as-sprayed and annealed states to assess recovery of functional behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the coatings showed martensitic transformation, thermal actuation in 3-point bending, and shape-memory effects in nanoindentation and scratch tests despite microstructural defects.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are defect-rich and must be annealed to achieve functional thermomechanical properties, which may add processing cost and complexity.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
